context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Right

episode_text:
day=5; meal=strawberries with whipped cream; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Right; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, potatoes, or hash browns THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected substantial reheating for observed hot meals in attention-occupied contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken and hash browns THEN 3 min BECAUSE the observed TV meal also showed a preference for thoroughly reheated hot food. IF food is fruit, dessert, or another cold-served item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, or task-focused THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold items such as fruit and strawberries with whipped cream were explicitly preferred without reheating across contexts.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a front-facing focal activity such as watching TV, working on a laptop in front, or interacting with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred preserving the front view in front-focused contexts. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the right such as watching TV on the right or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly selected preserving the right-side view in multiple right-focused contexts. No supported evidence yet for a stable left-specific occlusion preference.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition such as fruit pieces, strawberries, or hot solid foods AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social or task-focused work contexts THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option across several observed feeding meals. IF food is a long narrow solid item such as chicken strips AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user explicitly selected parallel skewering in the observed chicken-strip TV meal, suggesting food-shape sensitivity rather than a broad context shift.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN yes BECAUSE the user accepted explicit web confirmation in observed social settings, indicating a reliability preference despite extra interaction time. IF dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is straightforward fork-fed food including strawberries or simple hot solids THEN no BECAUSE the user selected skipping confirmation in these divided-attention contexts, consistent with preferring less interruption. Across observed contexts, the tie-break favors reliability in social settings but lower interaction burden in passive or occupied settings.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF food is being transferred for feeding AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward due to limited trunk control, and they consistently selected inside-mouth transfer across observed meals.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user consistently selected inside-mouth transfer and their physical profile supports that mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused such as eating with another person, working on a laptop, or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal readiness cue across observed contexts, consistent with reducing conversational disruption and avoiding unnecessary audio while attention is directed elsewhere.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation in the observed social meal. IF feeding task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred more automation when attention was occupied. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is toward automation under divided attention, while still avoiding button-based control due to physical limitations.

IF drinking task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for drinking in the observed social meal. IF drinking task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for wiping in the observed social meal. IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused such as eating with another person, working on a laptop, or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal cue when the tool reached transfer position, suggesting a stable preference for quiet visual-only completion signaling.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, which fits their inability to reliably press buttons.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal activity that the user wants to keep visible, including in front or to the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in social, laptop-working, TV, and right-laptop contexts.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred limited dipping in a social meal, consistent with reducing messiness. IF food includes a dip or sauce or topping AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping or extra coating in observed laptop meals, including strawberries with whipped cream, suggesting a stronger mess-avoidance preference when occupied. IF food has no dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, or entertainment-focused THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable and observed no-sauce meals also used do-not-dip.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in the observed social meal, balancing efficiency with enough time to respond using assistive interaction methods. IF dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a much longer wait when attention was divided by another focal activity, indicating a stable tie-break toward giving themselves ample intervention time during distraction.
